title: LIBS Mapping of Mg/Ca ratios
author: Niklas Hausmann
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.zc9f2z6

steps.title: ISOMET 1000 Precision Saw (Saw, Buehler,...
steps.instruction: ISOMET 1000 Precision Saw (Saw, Buehler, 11-2180, Using a Buehler Diamond Wafering Blade (Series 15LC Diamond No. 11-4276), https://s3.amazonaws.com/pr-journal/bepxa.png ) Section shells at the hinge and along the direction of growth. If necessary remove non-hinge parts of the section to reduce the overall size. Select the 'better' side of the two sections and clean off with Water Contributed by users and Ethanol Contributed by users Prepare a rudimentary holder ('Vesselheim') using crumpled up aluminium foil ( and cradle the shell half into it, so that the section is facing up and is near-horizontal XYZ Stage (Translation stage, STANDA, (Standa 8MT200-100DCE)) Place the sample into the centre of the xyz stage

steps.title: (Q-switched Nd:YAG Laser, Spectron Laser Systems,...
steps.instruction: (Q-switched Nd:YAG Laser, Spectron Laser Systems, n/a) Our LIBS system used a q-switched Nd:YAG laser operating at 1064 nm (infrared). Pulse duration was 10 ns and each pulse had an energy of ~10 mJ. LMH-10x (objctive lens, Thorlabs, LMH-10X-1064, https://www.thorlabs.com/newgrouppage9.cfm?objectgroup_id=4243&pn=LMH-10X-1064 ) Using an objective lens for infrared light, with a magnification of 10, we focused the laser beam onto the surface of the shell, creating an in-situ plasma plume and sampling an area of ~50µm. Czerny-Turner Spectrograph (TRIAX320, Jobin Yvon, n/a) Following irradiation, the plasma plume emitted light which we collected using a quartz fibre, which guides the light into a spectrograph using a 600 l/nm grating. DH520-18F (ICCD, Andor Technology, n/a) The light exits the spectrograph onto the sensor of an ICCD, which is synchronized with the Q-switch of the laser and gated using a digital delay pulse generator (DG535, Andor Technology). We used a delay of 500 ns and a gating of 1000 ns. LabView National Instruments Using a customised setup in LabView, we measured the peaks of the spectrum measured by the ICCD using the peak at 279.553 nm for MgII and 315.887 nm for CaII. The resulting ratio was associated with the location of the xyz-stage and saved as a csv file.

steps.title: QGIS The xy- values of the...
steps.instruction: QGIS The xy- values of the csv file were used to plot a point cloud in QGIS. Each point was then coloured by their respective Mg/Ca intensity ratio, resulting in an elemental map of the shell section.
